tagline: Fast, reliable repair for leaks, storm damage, and worn sections.
metaDescription: Roof repair in Los Angeles County — leak repair, storm damage, flashing, and emergency tarps. Free inspections. CA Lic. #1109337.
intro: Not every roof problem needs a full replacement. Palm Crest Roofing fixes leaks, damaged flashing, blown-off shingles, cracked tiles, storm damage, and pinhole leaks on flat roofs — often the same week you call. If your roof has good life left, we'll repair it honestly rather than push a replacement you don't need.
benefits: Leak diagnosis — pinpoint the actual source, not just visible damage
Asphalt shingle, tile, metal, and flat-roof repair
Flashing repair at walls, chimneys, skylights, and valleys
Honest assessments — we'll tell you if a repair doesn't make sense

process.title: Leak Inspection
process.desc: Roof walk + attic inspection to find the actual source. Leaks often travel along rafters before dripping — the visible drip rarely shows the real problem.

process.title: Written Quote
process.desc: Clear estimate with options: targeted repair, full section replacement, or full re-roof if needed.

process.title: Repair
process.desc: Flashing, shingle, tile, membrane, or penetration repair done to code with matching materials.

process.title: Verification
process.desc: Water test on completed repair. Photo documentation of work for your records and any insurance claim.

process.title: Follow-Up
process.desc: We stand behind every repair. If it leaks again within the warranty period, we come back and fix it.

faqs.q: Can you tarp my roof today?
faqs.a: During storms we prioritize emergency tarps and temporary repairs. Call 818.252.9422 and we'll get someone to you as fast as possible.

faqs.q: How much does a roof repair cost?
faqs.a: Targeted repairs (single flashing, shingle, or tile) run $300–$900. Larger section repairs run $1,000–$3,000. Flat-roof seam repairs vary by extent. Every repair gets a written quote before we start.

faqs.q: Will a repair void my manufacturer warranty?
faqs.a: Not when done properly with matching materials and to manufacturer spec. We follow manufacturer installation guidelines on every repair.
